stories.title: Dockable / collapsible panel layout
stories.description: Implement a dockable, resizable, collapsible panel system using AvalonDock so all views (log, agents, soundboard, now-playing) can be arranged by the user and persisted across sessions.
references: Â§4 Panel Layout System
Â§5.1 Core Shell — SHELL-01
Â§8 Persistence (Panel layout → layout.xml)

stories.title: System tray integration
stories.description: App minimizes to the system tray. Right-click context menu exposes: show/hide window, mute all, exit.
references: Â§5.1 Core Shell — SHELL-02

stories.title: Dockable mini-mode strip
stories.description: A compact floating strip showing per-agent status lights and master volume. Can be docked to a screen edge via Win32 AppBar or float freely.
references: Â§5.1 Core Shell — SHELL-03

stories.title: Configurable global hotkey bindings
stories.description: In-app UI for binding keyboard shortcuts to actions: mute all, toggle/solo a machine, force-play an agent, toggle an agent, switch profile, show/hide window. Uses RegisterHotKey via P/Invoke. Per-machine hotkey bindings override global app-level bindings when that machine is active.
references: Â§5.1 Core Shell — SHELL-04
Â§7 Hotkey Architecture

stories.title: Missing agent.ico breaks build
stories.description: AmbientSFXMachineGUI.csproj references ApplicationIcon=agent.ico but the file is absent from the repo, causing CS7064 during build. Either add the icon or remove the csproj reference.
references: Â§2 Technology Decision (NuGet dependencies, build setup)

stories.title: Agent status cards
stories.description: One card per agent showing: name, mode, enabled state, file count, last played file, next-play countdown timer, and turbo mode badge. Bound to AgentViewModel via INotifyPropertyChanged.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard UI elements
Â§3 Architecture (AgentViewModel exposes)

stories.title: Per-agent enable/disable toggle
stories.description: Toggle switch on each agent card to enable or disable the agent at runtime without restarting the application.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard: Toggle switch → IsEnabled

stories.title: Per-agent volume slider
stories.description: Live volume slider on each agent card that takes effect immediately without restarting.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard: Volume slider → Volume
Â§3.1 Machine concept (volume chain)

stories.title: Master volume and mute
stories.description: Global volume slider and mute toggle in the main toolbar that scales all machines simultaneously. Machine and agent volumes multiply into the app master: final = appMaster * machine.MasterVolume * agent.Volume * sound.VolumeOverride.
references: Â§4 Panel Layout System (Toolbar: App Master Vol | Mute All)
Â§3.1 Machine concept (volume chain)

stories.title: Force-play button per agent
stories.description: Button on each agent card that immediately triggers the agent's next sound, bypassing the current wait timer.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard: Force-play button

stories.title: In-app config editor
stories.description: Slide-over form editor for all agent .config values (intervals, mode, volume, balance, turbo settings) with validation. Saves back to disk and hot-applies to the running SoundAgent.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — Config Editor (slide-over sheet, not a modal)

stories.title: Min/max interval range sliders
stories.description: Replace raw minute/second config fields in the config editor with a dual-handle range slider for the interval window.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — Config Editor (Dual-handle RangeSlider for min/max intervals)

stories.title: Balance visualizer
stories.description: Small L/R panning arc indicator on each agent card that updates to reflect where the last sound was panned.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard: L/R balance arc indicator → LastPanValue

stories.title: Turbo mode indicator
stories.description: Animated pulse badge on the agent card when turbo mode is active, showing remaining turbo play count.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard: Turbo badge (animated pulse ring) → IsInTurboMode, RemainingTurboPlays

stories.title: Drag-and-drop agent folder import
stories.description: User can drag a folder onto a machine to register it as a new agent in that machine without manual config editing.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard UI
Â§5.8 Machine Management (Import machine — folder referenced in place)

stories.title: Machine data model and coordinator
stories.description: Introduce MachineViewModel (Id, Name, IconPath, IsEnabled, MasterVolume, RootPath, Agents) and MachineCoordinator owning ObservableCollection<MachineViewModel>. Refactor AgentCoordinator so its Agents collection lives on a MachineViewModel; playback/log/active-playback state stays on the coordinator (now MachineCoordinator) since the log is app-global.
references: Â§3 Architecture (MachineCoordinator, AgentCoordinator, MachineViewModel)
Â§3.1 Machine concept
Â§6 State Management

stories.title: Machines rail and machine card UI
stories.description: Left-edge dockable rail showing a vertical list of machine cards. Each card shows icon, name, enable toggle, master volume slider, and expands to reveal its agents. Selecting a machine scopes the agent panel to it.
references: Â§4 Panel Layout System (Default layout — Machines rail left edge)
Â§5.8 Machine Management (Machines rail)

stories.title: Per-machine master volume and group enable
stories.description: Implement the multiplier volume chain: final = appMaster * machine.MasterVolume * agent.Volume * sound.VolumeOverride. Disabling a machine pauses all of its agents as a group without restarting them.
references: Â§3.1 Machine concept (volume chain, disable behavior)
Â§5.8 Machine Management (machine card: enable toggle, master volume slider)

stories.title: Import existing console-app machine folder
stories.description: File > Import Machine picker that accepts a directory containing appSettings.config and snd/. Reads appName and icon from appSettings.config, then scans snd/<agent>/*.config to materialize AgentViewModels. References the folder in place (no copying). Validate against C:\Users\homel\OneDrive\Projects\SpookyAudioMachine, ThursdayGameNightAudioSFX, and ThursdayPacmanAudioSFX. Validation/error-handling scope (added during testing): reject folders that are not a valid machine root with a clear, non-modal GUI error explaining what is missing — specifically detect and message the common mistake of selecting the snd/ subfolder instead of the machine root (i.e. the picked folder IS named 'snd' OR lacks appSettings.config but contains agent-style subfolders with .config files) and suggest 'select the parent folder instead'. Other rejection cases: missing appSettings.config, missing snd/ subfolder, snd/ has no agent subfolders, appSettings.config unreadable/malformed. On rejection: do NOT add a partial/broken MachineViewModel to the coordinator (this is what produced the iconless 'snd' ghost machine), do not persist anything, and surface the error both via a non-blocking toast/dialog and DebugLog.LogError. Re-importing the same valid machine root should be idempotent (match on RootPath) rather than creating a duplicate.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Import machine (MachineImporter.cs)

stories.title: Create new empty machine
stories.description: Dialog to create a new machine: prompts for name, icon file, and root folder (new or existing empty directory). Creates the machine metadata and an empty snd/ subfolder.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Create machine

stories.title: Rename, change icon, and reorder machines
stories.description: Context menu and drag-reorder on machine cards. Rename updates the persisted index; icon picker accepts .ico/.png and stores path relative to machine root when possible.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Rename / change icon / reorder

stories.title: Remove machine (unlink, do not delete files)
stories.description: Remove action on a machine card that deletes the machine's entry from the index and unhooks its agents/library usages. Warns explicitly that audio files and .config files on disk are not touched.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Remove machine

stories.title: Persist machine index to %AppData%
stories.description: Serialize MachineCoordinator state to %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<id>.json (id, name, iconPath, isEnabled, masterVolume, rootPath, order). Restore on startup. Agent/sound configs remain at RootPath and are not duplicated here.
references: Â§8 Persistence (Machine index → %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<id>.json)

stories.title: System tray submenu per machine
stories.description: Extend TrayService so each machine appears as a submenu in the tray context menu with its own icon and entries: toggle mute, solo (mute all others), show cards.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Per-machine tray submenu

stories.title: Scope profiles, soundboard, and hotkeys per-machine
stories.description: Move profile/soundboard/hotkey storage under %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<machineId>\ so each machine owns its own. Profiles apply only to their parent machine; soundboard buttons and hotkey bindings are machine-scoped. Global app-level hotkeys (mute all, show window) remain in the app-level hotkeys.json.
references: Â§8 Persistence (Profiles, Soundboard, Hotkeys — per-machine paths)

stories.title: AudioFileEntry and AudioLibrary registry
stories.description: Introduce AudioFileEntry (AbsolutePath, Sha256, Duration, ByteSize, FileName, Usages) and AudioLibrary as a process-wide singleton keyed by content hash with a secondary index by absolute path. Usages list is maintained by subscribing to mutations on every MachineViewModel.Agents[*].Files collection.

Â§6 State Management (AudioLibrary singleton)

stories.title: Background SHA-256 hasher with cache
stories.description: Stream each referenced audio file through SHA-256 on a background thread; cache results in %AppData%\AmbientAgents\library.json keyed by path with file size+mtime invalidation. Surface a progress indicator for initial scans.
references: Â§3.2 Audio Library (Hashing: lazy, streamed SHA-256)
Â§8 Persistence (Library cache → library.json)
Â§5.9 Audio Library (progress indicator in tab header)

stories.title: Audio duration and size metadata probe
stories.description: Probe audio duration via NAudio (AudioFileReader.TotalTime) and file size; cache in library.json alongside the hash. Required for duration-based likely-duplicate matching.
references: Â§3.2 Audio Library (entry: Duration, ByteSize probed via NAudio)
Â§5.9 Audio Library — Duplicates tab (likely duplicates: duration within ±1%)

stories.title: Library panel: Duplicates tab

Profiles are scoped per-machine and saved under %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<machineId>\profiles\. Load restores all settings for that machine.

Â§8 Persistence (Profiles per-machine → %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<id>\profiles\*.json)

stories.description: Switch between profiles at runtime from a toolbar dropdown or hotkey. All settings apply immediately without restart. Skips any agent marked as pinned.

Â§6 State Management (ProfileService.Apply iterates agents, raises ProfileChanged)

Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard UI elements

stories.title: Black background makes text unreadable in Now Playing and agent file lists
stories.description: Text rendered in the Now Playing panel (active sounds list) and in the agent detail/file list rows has a black background, making it invisible against dark text or dark themes. Repro: import a machine, observe the now-playing entries and the expanded agent file list. Expected: list item backgrounds should inherit the panel/theme background so text is legible. Screenshot captured at Screenshots/S-BUG-002 Black background 2026-04-24 070204.png.
references: Â§4 Panel Layout System
Â§5.5 Now Playing Panel
Â§5.3 Sound File Management

stories.title: Group agents by machine in collapsible sections in the agents panel
stories.description: The agent cards panel currently shows a flat list of all agents with no indication of which machine they belong to. When multiple machines are imported, agents from different machines are indistinguishable. Wrap each machine's agents in a collapsible GroupBox or Expander section whose header shows the machine name (and optionally its icon). Expanding/collapsing a section should persist across sessions. This is the prerequisite for MACHINE-12 (per-machine play/pause).
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management (Machines rail)
Â§5.2 Agent Management — AgentCard UI elements

stories.title: Importing a second machine clears agent list instead of appending, and crashes on retry
stories.description: When a second machine folder is imported via File > Import Machine, the agent panel is cleared rather than having the new machine's agents appended alongside the first machine's agents. Re-importing the same second machine after the clear causes a crash (exception not yet captured — reproduce and record stack trace). Expected: each imported machine's agents should be added to MachineCoordinator independently; the UI should show all machines' agents grouped by machine (see MACHINE-11). The clear-on-second-import and the crash are likely the same root cause (duplicate machine ID or collection mutation on the wrong thread).
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Import machine (MachineImporter.cs)
Â§3 Architecture (MachineCoordinator, ObservableCollection<MachineViewModel>)

stories.title: Session debug log panel
stories.description: Add a dedicated debug/session log panel (separate from the live playback log) that captures three categories of events in a scrolling, timestamped list: (1) User actions — import machine, toggle agent, switch profile, adjust volume, press hotkey, etc. (2) Agent activity — which sound each agent selected, when it fired, its volume/pan values, any skip/cooldown decisions. (3) Application errors — caught exceptions, failed file reads, playback engine errors, and crash reports with stack traces. Each entry shows: timestamp, category badge (USER / AGENT / ERROR), source (machine+agent name where applicable), and message. Entries are colour-coded by category. The panel is dockable, defaults to the bottom dock beside the playback log, and caps at 2000 entries in-memory. Disk persistence: log entries are written to a rolling .log file under %AppData%\AmbientAgents\logs\ (default location, one file per session named by start timestamp). Write strategy: buffer in memory and flush to disk every 1000 new entries; also flush on clean shutdown via Application.Exit. For crash persistence, register an UnhandledException handler on AppDomain.CurrentDomain and a DispatcherUnhandledException handler on the WPF Application that synchronously flush the buffer before the process dies — this is best-effort (OOM or hard kill cannot be caught). Log panel toolbar contains: (a) 'Change log folder' button — opens a FolderBrowserDialog and persists the chosen path to app settings; (b) 'Open log folder' button — opens the current log folder in Windows Explorer via Process.Start. A 'Copy to clipboard' button copies the visible in-memory entries. The save location persists in %AppData%\AmbientAgents\settings.json under a debugLogPath key.
references: Â§5.4 Live Playback Log
Â§4 Panel Layout System (tabbed bottom dock)

stories.title: Play/pause button per machine
stories.description: Add a dedicated play/pause toggle button to each machine's group header in the agents panel (introduced by MACHINE-11). Pause stops all active playback for that machine's agents and suspends their scheduling timers; Play resumes from the paused state. This is distinct from the enable/disable toggle (MACHINE-03): pause is a temporary suspension for the session whereas disable is a persistent state change. The button should show a Play icon when paused and a Pause icon when running.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management
Â§3.1 Machine concept (disable behavior)

stories.title: Tray submenu: Close (unload) machine action
stories.description: Extend the per-machine system tray submenu (added by MACHINE-09) with a 'Close machine' entry that unloads the machine from the running app — stops its agents, removes it from MachineCoordinator, and drops its tray submenu. This is the unload-from-session counterpart to MACHINE-07 (Remove machine, which also unlinks from the persisted index); Close should keep the machine in the recent/previously-loaded list (see MACHINE-15) so it can be re-imported later with one click. Confirm via a lightweight tray-balloon or inline submenu prompt rather than a modal. Also surfaces the same issue encountered during testing: a broken 'snd' pseudo-machine (see MACHINE-04 validation) currently shows in the tray with no icon and cannot be removed — this story must ensure Close works even for degraded/iconless entries.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Per-machine tray submenu
Â§5.1 Core Shell — SHELL-02

stories.title: Audio Machines dockable window — shell and loaded machines section
stories.description: Add a new dockable panel titled 'Audio Machines' that becomes the central hub for machine lifecycle management (parallel to the Library panel for files). This story delivers the panel shell plus the 'Loaded' section: a list/grid of currently loaded MachineViewModels rendered with a green-tinted background to indicate active state. Each row shows icon, machine name, a 'Close' button that unloads the machine (same semantics as MACHINE-13), and a stats line 'N agents · M enabled' computed from MachineViewModel.Agents (total count and count where IsEnabled==true, updating live via INotifyPropertyChanged on each agent). The panel header has a toolbar area reserved for the buttons delivered by MACHINE-16 (Create) and MACHINE-18 (Unload all). Dock defaults to the left or right side alongside the Machines rail; layout persists via the existing AvalonDock layout.xml.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management
Â§4 Panel Layout System

stories.title: Audio Machines window — previously loaded / recent machines section
stories.description: Add a 'Recent' (a.k.a. previously loaded) section to the Audio Machines panel (MACHINE-14) rendered with a gray-tinted background. Source: a new persisted recent-machines list at %AppData%\AmbientAgents\recent-machines.json capturing each machine that has ever been loaded (RootPath, last known name, iconPath, lastLoadedAt, lastKnownAgentCount). Updated on every successful import (MACHINE-04) and on Close (MACHINE-13). Each row shows icon, name, 'Last loaded: <date>', stats line 'Had N agents last session', a primary 'Load' button that re-imports via MachineImporter using the stored RootPath, and a 'Forget' button that removes the entry from recent-machines.json (does not touch files on disk). If the stored RootPath no longer exists on disk, mark the row with a warning glyph and disable the Load button but keep Forget enabled.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management
Â§8 Persistence

stories.title: Audio Machines window — Create new machine action
stories.description: Add a 'Create new machine' button to the Audio Machines panel toolbar (MACHINE-14). Clicking opens a dialog that prompts for: (1) machine name (required, validated non-empty and filesystem-safe), (2) parent directory to host the new machine folder (FolderBrowserDialog, required), (3) icon file — an OpenFileDialog filtered to .ico/.png/.exe/.dll whose InitialDirectory defaults to %SystemRoot%\System32 (Windows system icons folder) for convenient browsing of system icons. On confirm, the app creates <parent>\<name>\ with an empty snd\ subfolder and a minimal appSettings.config, then imports the newly created machine via the same MachineImporter path used by MACHINE-04 so it immediately appears in the Loaded section. Icon handling (IMPORTANT — do not just store the source path): the chosen icon file must be *copied into the new machine's folder* so the machine stays self-contained and survives the source being moved/deleted. Copy rules by extension: (a) .ico or .png — File.Copy into <machineRoot>\icon.<ext>; (b) .exe or .dll — cannot be used as an icon directly because PE binaries carry icons as embedded Win32 resources, not as image bytes, so the app must extract the icon and write it as a real .ico inside the machine folder: use System.Drawing.Icon.ExtractAssociatedIcon(sourcePath) for the default associated icon, or ExtractIconEx via P/Invoke (shell32.dll) if selecting a specific resource index is desired, then icon.Save(targetStream) to <machineRoot>\icon.ico. If extraction returns null or fails, show a clear error and leave the machine iconless rather than storing a broken reference. In all cases appSettings.config's icon field stores a path *relative to machineRoot* (e.g. 'icon.ico'). Overlaps in intent with MACHINE-05 (Create new empty machine); this story is the Audio-Machines-panel entry point and should reuse the MACHINE-05 creation logic, and MACHINE-05 should be extended with the same copy-and-extract icon rules if it does not already do so.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Create machine

stories.title: Audio Machines window — edit loaded machine (name, icon)
stories.description: Add an 'Edit' action to each Loaded row in the Audio Machines panel (MACHINE-14) that opens a dialog to change the machine's display name and icon path. Persist changes to the machine's appSettings.config on disk AND to the %AppData% machine index (MACHINE-08). Because the library/soundboard/profile layers key some records by machine name/id, the rename path must cascade: update every library usage's machine-display-name, soundboard/profile folder under %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<id>\ (rename directory if id-derived-from-name, else leave id stable and just update display name), and any hotkey binding labels that embed the machine name. Call out in the story's acceptance criteria that icon changes are purely cosmetic but a rename touches library/mapping and must not orphan sound file usages. Overlaps with MACHINE-06 (Rename/change icon/reorder machines); this is the Audio-Machines-panel entry point and should reuse MACHINE-06 logic.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management — Rename / change icon / reorder

stories.title: Audio Machines window — Unload all machines button
stories.description: Add an 'Unload all' button to the Audio Machines panel toolbar (MACHINE-14) that closes every currently loaded machine in one action (per-machine unload uses the same path as MACHINE-13). Prompt once for confirmation since this is a bulk, non-destructive but disruptive action. After unload, all machines appear in the Recent section (MACHINE-15) and must be re-imported via File > Import Machine or the Recent section's Load button to become active again. No on-disk files are touched.
references: Â§5.8 Machine Management

stories.title: Rename agent within a machine
stories.description: Add a 'Rename' action to the agent card (context menu and/or inline edit on the name label) that changes the agent's display name and its on-disk folder name under <machineRoot>\snd\<agent>\. Renaming the folder requires rewriting every library usage that references files under the old path (AudioLibrary.Usages entries + any file-override paths stored in profiles and soundboard items under %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<id>\). Acceptance criteria: (1) rename is rejected with a clear error if the target folder name already exists or is filesystem-invalid; (2) any currently playing sound from that agent is stopped gracefully before the folder rename; (3) library/soundboard/profile mappings are rewritten atomically so no reference is orphaned; (4) DebugLog.LogUser records the rename with old → new names.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management
Â§3.2 Audio Library (Usages)
Â§8 Persistence

stories.title: Reorder agents within a machine
stories.description: Allow the user to reorder agent cards within a machine via drag-and-drop on the card header (or up/down buttons as fallback). Order is persisted per-machine to the machine index JSON under %AppData%\AmbientAgents\machines\<id>.json (new 'agentOrder' field: array of agent folder names). On import, if agentOrder is absent or contains stale entries, fall back to alphabetical order and reconcile. Ordering is purely presentational and must not affect scheduling, cooldowns, or library references.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management
Â§8 Persistence

stories.title: Create new agent within a machine
stories.description: Add a 'New agent' action on the machine header (in the Machines rail and/or Audio Machines window) that creates a new agent inside that machine. Dialog prompts for agent name (required, filesystem-safe, unique within the machine's snd\). On confirm, create <machineRoot>\snd\<name>\ and a minimal <name>.config populated with sensible defaults (same defaults MachineImporter expects: mode, min/max interval, volume, balance). The new agent appears live in the agent panel as an empty-file AgentViewModel ready to accept drag-drop audio (AGENT-10) or manual file additions. DebugLog.LogUser records the creation. Overlaps in spirit with AGENT-10 (drag-drop folder import) but this entry point is name-first rather than files-first.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management
Â§5.8 Machine Management

stories.title: Agents produce no audio when enabled or on Force Play
stories.description: After importing a machine, no sound is ever heard. The agent 'On' toggle is blue (IsEnabled true) and clicking 'Force Play' on an agent row also produces no audio. Debug Log / Live Log may or may not record playback decisions — verify both paths. Expected: toggling an agent On should schedule playback per its mode/interval and emit audio via the playback engine; Force Play should immediately pick a file and play it through the output device. Investigate: (a) whether AgentViewModel/AgentCoordinator scheduler actually fires, (b) whether MachineCoordinator routes playback to the audio engine, (c) whether the NAudio (or equivalent) output device is initialized, (d) master/per-agent volume multiplication isn't collapsing to 0, (e) file paths resolved from the imported machine actually exist on disk. Confirm a regression window by testing a previously-known-good build if needed.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management
Â§6 Playback Engine
Â§5.5 Now Playing Panel

stories.title: Now Playing panel crashes app on first activation (TwoWay binding to TimeSpan.TotalMilliseconds)
stories.description: After S-BUG-004, audio plays as expected, but clicking the pinned 'Now Playing' tab in the bottom dock crashes the app. Captured stack from %AppData%\AmbientAgents\logs: 'A TwoWay or OneWayToSource binding cannot work on the read-only property TotalMilliseconds of type System.TimeSpan' raised from XamlParseException during the panel's first measure pass. Cause: Panels/NowPlayingPanel/NowPlayingPanelView.xaml binds ProgressBar.Value to {Binding Position.TotalMilliseconds} without Mode=OneWay; ProgressBar inherits RangeBase.Value which has FrameworkPropertyMetadataOptions.BindsTwoWayByDefault, so WPF tries to write back to TimeSpan.TotalMilliseconds (read-only) and throws on first render. Fix: explicitly set Mode=OneWay on the ProgressBar Value and Maximum bindings (and on any other read-only-target bindings in the panel).
references: Â§5.5 Now Playing Panel

stories.title: Soundboard 'Stop All' button stops only soundboard-triggered sounds
stories.description: Add a 'Stop All' button to the Soundboard panel toolbar that immediately stops every sound that was triggered manually via the soundboard (button click or assigned hotkey) without affecting sounds currently playing from normal agent/machine scheduling or Force Play. Implementation notes: tag each playback started by the soundboard with a source marker (e.g. PlaybackSource.Soundboard) when it enters the active-playback list; the Stop All command iterates ActivePlaybacks and stops only entries whose source == Soundboard; agent-scheduled and Force Play entries remain untouched. DebugLog.LogUser records the action with count stopped. Button should be disabled when no soundboard-sourced sounds are currently playing.
references: Â§5.6 Soundboard / Favorites
Â§5.5 Now Playing Panel
Â§6 Playback Engine

stories.title: Agents panel is empty at startup until an agent is re-imported
stories.description: On app launch the 'Agents' dock panel renders an empty list even though agents from the previously-loaded machines are alive: their schedulers fire and play audio, and the system tray menu enumerates them. Root cause (confirmed from session log): the user's MachinesDir contains multiple machine records, one of which is an empty 'Default' machine at Order=0 with no RootPath (and therefore zero agents). ShellViewModel's ctor hard-selected Machines[0], so the Default empty machine became SelectedMachine; meanwhile the populated PACMAN/Spooky machines (Order>=1) still had their AgentCoordinators running in the background, which is why audio kept playing. Agents panel bound correctly — it was just bound to the empty Default machine's Agents collection. Dropping an agent folder onto the panel appeared to 'fix' things because ImportAgentFolder adds to SelectedMachine (Default), and with the collection-mirror refactor any new add is reflected — but the underlying bug was that the wrong machine was selected from the start. Fix: (a) ShellViewModel.PickInitialMachine() — prefer the last-selected machine id persisted in AppSettings.lastSelectedMachineId; otherwise pick the first machine with Agents.Count>0 (skipping empty auto-created Default); fall back to Machines[0]. (b) Persist SelectedMachine.Id to AppSettings whenever it changes so subsequent sessions restore the user's pick. (c) As a defense-in-depth cleanup (separate from the root cause), convert ShellViewModel.Agents / SoundboardItems from switching proxies into stable ObservableCollections mirrored from SelectedMachine via CollectionChanged forwarding, so the view binds once and never risks latching onto a stale empty reference even if SelectedMachine is briefly null during init.
references: Â§5.2 Agent Management
Â§4 Panel Layout System
